info.name: Cookies without Secure attribute - Detect
author: vthiery
rj coleman
info.description: Checks whether cookies in the HTTP response contain the Secure attribute. If the Secure flag is set, it means that the cookie can only be transmitted over HTTPS
info.impact: Lack of Secure flag on cookies allows the cookie to be sent over unsecure HTTP, making it vulnerable to man-in-the-middle (MITM) attacks.
info.remediation: Ensure that all cookies are set with the Secure attribute to prevent MITM attacks.

response: [_fs_ch_st_FSBmUei20MqUiJb9]

info.name: WAF Detection
author: dwisiswant0
lu4nx
info.description: A web application firewall was detected.

request:
POST / HTTP/1.1
Host: flyeverywhere.kiwi.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux aarch64; rv:90.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/90.0
Connection: close
Content-Length: 27
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded
Accept-Encoding: gzip

_=<script>alert(1)</script>
response:
HTTP/1.1 403 Forbidden
Connection: close
Transfer-Encoding: chunked
Accept-Ranges: bytes
Alt-Svc: h3=":443";ma=86400,h3-29=":443";ma=86400,h3-27=":443";ma=86400
Cache-Control: private, no-store
Date: Sat, 21 Mar 2026 11:55:01 GMT
Via: 1.1 varnish
X-Cache: MISS
X-Cache-Hits: 0
X-Served-By: cache-qau-vaah4730024-QAU, cache-qau-vaah4730028-QAU
curl-command: curl -X 'POST' -d '_=<script>alert(1)</script>' -H 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' -H 'Host: flyeverywhere.kiwi.com' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (X11; Ubuntu; Linux aarch64; rv:90.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/90.0' 'https://flyeverywhere.kiwi.com'

info.name: TLS Version - Detect
author: pdteam
pussycat0x
info.description:
TLS version detection is a security process used to determine the version of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol used by a computer or server.
It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.

info.name: TLS Version - Detect
author: pdteam
pussycat0x
info.description:
TLS version detection is a security process used to determine the version of the Transport Layer Security (TLS) protocol used by a computer or server.
It is important to detect the TLS version in order to ensure secure communication between two computers or servers.

author: lucky0x0d

info.name: Fastly CDN Debug Headers Exposure
author: pussycat0x
info.description: Detected Fastly CDN debug headers being exposed when the Fastly-Debug header was sent in a request.This exposure disclosed sensitive debugging information such as cache paths, TTL values, content digests, surrogate keys, and cache server identities, which could help attackers understand CDN configuration and cache behavior.

request:
GET / HTTP/1.1
Host: flyeverywhere.kiwi.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.15; rv:134.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/134.0
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Fastly-Debug: 1
Accept-Encoding: gzip
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'Fastly-Debug: 1' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Macintosh; Intel Mac OS X 10.15; rv:134.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/134.0' 'https://flyeverywhere.kiwi.com'

info.name: Android Asset Links Configuration - Detect
author: rxerium
info.description: The .well-known/assetlinks.json file was found on the target server. This file is used by Android applications to establish verified app-to-web domain associations through the Digital Asset Links protocol.

request:
GET /.well-known/assetlinks.json HTTP/1.1
Host: flyeverywhere.kiwi.com
User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; WOW64; rv:41.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/140.0 (x64 de)
Connection: close
Accept: */*
Accept-Language: en
Accept-Encoding: gzip
curl-command: curl -X 'GET' -d '' -H 'Accept: */*' -H 'Accept-Language: en' -H 'User-Agent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64; WOW64; rv:41.0) Gecko/20100101 Firefox/140.0 (x64 de)' 'https://flyeverywhere.kiwi.com/.well-known/assetlinks.json'

info.name: CAA Record
author: pdteam
info.description: A CAA record was discovered. A CAA record is used to specify which certificate authorities (CAs) are allowed to issue certificates for a domain.

request:
;; opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 48414
;; flags: rd; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 0, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version 0; flags:; udp: 4096

;; QUESTION SECTION:
;flyeverywhere.kiwi.com.	IN	 CAA
response:
;; opcode: QUERY, status: NOERROR, id: 48414
;; flags: qr rd ra; QUERY: 1, ANSWER: 0, AUTHORITY: 1, ADDITIONAL: 1

;; OPT PSEUDOSECTION:
; EDNS: version 0; flags:; udp: 512

;; QUESTION SECTION:
;flyeverywhere.kiwi.com.	IN	 CAA

;; AUTHORITY SECTION:
kiwi.com.	300	IN	SOA	ns-cloud-c1.googledomains.com. cloud-dns-hostmaster.google.com. 1 21600 3600 259200 300

info.name: Detect SSL Certificate Issuer
author: lingtren
info.description: Extract the issuer's organization from the target's certificate. Issuers are entities which sign and distribute certificates.

info.name: SSL DNS Names
author: pdteam
info.description: Extract the Subject Alternative Name (SAN) from the target's certificate. SAN facilitates the usage of additional hostnames with the same certificate.

info.name: Wildcard TLS Certificate
author: lucky0x0d
info.description: Checks a sites certificate to see if there are wildcard CN or SAN entries.
